tasks.title: Phase 1 — Baseline gates & e2e contract re-grep
tasks.description: Establish a green baseline before any changes. Run `cd web && npm run build` (tsc -b + vite build) and `make build && make test` and record that the current tree compiles/tests green. Re-grep `web/e2e.mjs` and enumerate every exact-string and role/aria contract it asserts (button labels, headings, testids, roles) into a short note appended to `.jonggrang/progress.txt` so later phases add UI without renaming those anchors. Acceptance: build+test output captured as green (or existing failures documented); a written list of e2e-asserted anchors exists; no source files changed except progress.txt.

tasks.title: Phase 2 — Add shadcn primitives, sonner, Toaster mount & theme toggle
tasks.description: Add the missing shadcn/ui primitives and toast system. Install deps @radix-ui/react-dialog, @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu, @radix-ui/react-checkbox, @radix-ui/react-tooltip, sonner (update web/package.json + package-lock.json). Create web/src/components/ui/{dialog,dropdown-menu,checkbox,tooltip,skeleton,sonner}.tsx following existing shadcn conventions (cva variants, cn() merge, radix primitives, @/* alias). Mount <Toaster/> in web/src/App.tsx. Add a light/dark theme toggle in the App sidebar that flips the theme class on the root and persists to localStorage; ensure light-mode CSS variables + toast styling exist in web/src/styles.css. Acceptance: `cd web && npm run build` green; primitives importable via @/components/ui; theme toggle switches tokens; Toaster rendered once.

tasks.title: Phase 2 — Replace native alert/prompt/confirm with toasts + confirm dialogs
tasks.description: Remove every native alert()/prompt()/confirm() from web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx and web/src/pages/Settings.tsx (deploy/scale/rollback/delete and 2FA-disable flows). Replace success/error feedback with sonner toasts and replace destructive/confirmation prompts with the shadcn dialog confirm pattern. Swap the raw checkbox input for the shadcn Checkbox. Do NOT rename any e2e-asserted text/roles from task-001's list. Acceptance: grep for alert(/prompt(/confirm( in both pages returns nothing; confirm dialogs gate destructive actions; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 2 — Skeleton loaders & consistent empty/error states
tasks.description: Replace plain loading text with the shadcn Skeleton component and add consistent empty-state and error-state rendering + toast feedback across web/src/pages/Projects.tsx, web/src/pages/Cluster.tsx, web/src/pages/Audit.tsx, and web/src/pages/Auth.tsx. Keep e2e anchors intact. Acceptance: each listed page shows skeletons during load and a clear empty/error state; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 3 — Live-follow Logs tab (streaming fetch reader)
tasks.description: Add a streaming reader helper to web/src/api.ts (incremental fetch/ReadableStream with an AbortController) that consumes /projects/{p}/logs?follow=true. Rewrite the Logs tab in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx to stream live with auto-scroll, plus Stop and Clear controls; abort the reader on unmount and on Stop, and cap the buffer size to avoid unbounded memory. Acceptance: logs follow live and auto-scroll; Stop halts streaming and aborts the request; no leaked readers on tab switch/unmount; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 3 — Deployment build-log viewer drawer
tasks.description: Add a deployment detail drawer/dialog in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx that streams the build log for a running or finished deployment via /deployments/{id} (reusing the task-005 streaming helper in web/src/api.ts), so push repo to live URL is observable. Show deployment status and allow closing/aborting the stream. Acceptance: clicking a deployment opens a drawer streaming its build log; stream aborts on close; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 4 — Env-vars & Secrets editor (M4)
tasks.description: Add a per-app environment-variable key/value editor and a project/app Secrets editor in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx, wired to the /secrets CRUD endpoints and the app env/secretKeys fields. Add any additive helpers needed to web/src/api.ts (no breaking contract changes). Support add/edit/remove rows, masked secret values, and toast feedback on save. Acceptance: variables and secrets can be created/updated/deleted from the UI and persist via the API; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 4 — Domains/TLS management UI (M4)
tasks.description: Surface domain + TLS management in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx beyond the single create-form field: list domains for an app with host, TLS on/off toggle, and cert status; allow add/remove. Wire to existing app domain fields/endpoints via additive helpers in web/src/api.ts if needed. Acceptance: multiple domains manageable per app with TLS state and cert status shown; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 5 — Scale/rollback dialogs + autoscale/rollout fields (M6)
tasks.description: Replace prompt()-based scale/rollback with proper shadcn dialogs in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx (calling /projects/{p}/services/{s}/scale and /rollback). Expose the existing but unreachable app fields autoscaleMin/autoscaleMax/autoscaleCpu/autoscaleMemory and rollout in the app create/edit form UI so they can be set. Acceptance: scale and rollback run through dialogs (no prompt()); autoscale + rollout fields are editable and submitted in appReq; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 5 — CPU/RAM metric graphs/bars in Overview (M6)
tasks.description: Render CPU/RAM metrics as compact graphs/bars in the Overview of web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx instead of raw numbers in a table cell, consuming /projects/{p}/metrics (instantaneous). Prefer lightweight inline/CSS bars to avoid a new charting dependency; only add recharts if explicitly wanted. Acceptance: CPU and RAM display as visual bars/graphs with readable labels; no heavy new dep unless justified; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 6 — Backup cron schedule + S3 destination UI (M8)
tasks.description: Add backup cron schedule and S3 destination inputs to web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx, wired to /projects/{p}/backups (backend pkg/backup/s3.go already supports it). Allow configuring schedule, S3 bucket/credentials destination, and triggering/listing backups. Acceptance: backup schedule + S3 destination configurable and persisted via API; backup list visible; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 6 — Preview deploy trigger + teardown UI (M9)
tasks.description: Add preview-deployment trigger and teardown controls on the app row in web/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx, wired to /apps/{id}/preview and /projects/{p}/preview/{branch}. Show preview URL/status and allow teardown with a confirm dialog. Acceptance: preview deploy can be triggered and torn down from the UI with visible status; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 6 — Org member / RBAC management surface (M1, low-risk)
tasks.description: Add an org member / RBAC management surface (list members, roles, add/remove) in web/src/pages/Settings.tsx and, if needed, a nav entry in web/src/App.tsx, wired to existing member endpoints via additive helpers in web/src/api.ts. Pull in only if low-risk; if the backend endpoints are insufficient, document the gap in .jonggrang/progress.txt and defer rather than half-build. Acceptance: members listable with roles and add/remove works, OR a clear deferral note is recorded; npm run build green.

tasks.title: Phase 7 — Regenerate embedded dist, verify, atomic commit
tasks.description: Run `make ui` so pkg/webui/dist matches source (old bundle deleted, new bundle embedded via pkg/webui/embed.go — do not edit embed.go). Re-run `make build && make test` and confirm green. git add the new web/src/components/, web/src/lib/, new deps/lockfile, and the rebuilt pkg/webui/dist assets, then land atomic commit(s) per AGENTS.md commit prefix convention. Acceptance: embedded dist regenerated and staged; make build && make test green; changes committed atomically with a feat|fix|refactor|test|docs|chore(scope): message.

tasks.title: Phase 8 — Log learnings to progress.txt
tasks.description: Append to .jonggrang/progress.txt: the real audited M1-M9 state (what was already implemented vs genuinely missing), the fragility of the web/e2e.mjs exact-string/role contracts, and the embedded-dist sync rule (source changes require make ui or the binary serves stale UI). Acceptance: progress.txt contains these three learnings written clearly for the next session.
